name: Ken Brooks
occupation: Loss control safety specialist at Insurance Planning (18 years)
family: Single parent. Three daughters, all graduated from Hays High School.
whoTheyAre:
Ken Brooks is a longtime Hays resident who has spent most of his adult life raising his family and serving the community. He is a loss control safety specialist at Insurance Planning, where he has worked for 18 years. He is a single parent whose three daughters all graduated from Hays High School, giving him a deeply personal connection to the USD 489 school district.

Brooks is also one of the most active church volunteers in Hays. He has helped lead the children's ministry at Celebration Community Church for nearly 14 years and has coordinated the Thanksgiving Community Food Drive (through ECMA and First Call For Help) since 2008. He is a member of the Hays Kiwanis Club. People who follow his public record describe him as a listener who leads with service rather than politics.

He was first elected to the USD 489 school board in 2021 and won re-election in November 2025 with 1,948 votes. He has served as both board president and vice president. He says he ran because his experience as a parent in the district showed him ways the schools could improve, and he wanted to help make that happen.
theirRecord:
Brooks has been a steady, pro-administration voice on the USD 489 board. He supported the district's $143.5 million bond that funded the new Hays High School, the middle school conversion, and elementary school renovations. He has repeatedly pushed to lower student enrollment fees for families and says the district has followed through on that during his time on the board. He also backed the Golden Belt Bank naming rights deal for the football field, saying the bank had no "ill intentions" and was trying to help the community.

On oversight, Brooks takes a trust-but-verify approach. He has said the board should provide broad accountability while leaving day-to-day operations to Superintendent Ron Wilson and the district finance team. He called a special meeting as board president specifically because he wanted to show the board was "striving for transparency." He also challenged fellow board member Allen Park on repeated dissenting votes, asking what Park would have changed about a contract had he reviewed it in advance, suggesting the no votes were more about optics than substance.

One notable moment in his record: Brooks voted against changing the Hays Middle School mascot to "Indians" in a 5-2 vote, siding with the minority position. This showed a willingness to break from the board majority on a cultural issue. Overall, his record reflects a pragmatic board member who supports the administration's direction, pushes for lower costs for families, and avoids ideological fights.
whatYouShouldKnow: Brooks has helped lead the children's ministry at Celebration Community Church in Hays for nearly 14 years. This is hands-on, week-in-week-out volunteer work, not a token role.
He has coordinated the Thanksgiving Community Food Drive (through ECMA and First Call For Help) every year since 2008.
He supported the district's $143.5 million bond that built the new Hays High School and funded other school construction projects.
He has pushed to lower student enrollment fees and says fees have gone down during his time on the board.
He voted against changing the Hays Middle School mascot to "Indians" in a 5-2 vote, breaking from the board majority on that cultural issue.
In 2021, he said he had not received any outside money or PAC donations for his school board campaign.
He generally votes with the administration majority on the board, alongside Ruth Ruder and Jayme Goetz. Allen Park and Derek Yarmer form the main dissenting bloc on spending and oversight issues.
All three of his daughters have graduated from Hays High, and none are currently enrolled in the district.
whereTheyWorship:
Ken Brooks is a member of Celebration Community Church in Hays. He has not just attended there -- he has helped lead the children's ministry for nearly 14 years. Children's ministry is one of the most time-intensive and least glamorous volunteer roles in any church, and doing it for over a decade speaks to genuine commitment.

Celebration Community Church is a non-denominational, evangelical congregation with campuses in Hays and WaKeeney. The Hays campus is located at 2650 E. 8th Street. You can learn more about the church at https://www.celebratejesus.org/hays-campus.

church.name: Celebration Community Church
church.details: Children's ministry leader for nearly 14 years. Hays campus at 2650 E. 8th Street.

quotes.text: I have a love for our community, and I have a love for kids.
Since I've been on the board, we've lowered the school fees.
There hasn't been anything where our school district has proven they're not responsible with their budget.
I have helped lead the Children's Ministry at Celebration Community Church for almost 14 years.
